name: MMX
first: First sample return from a Martian moon
description: Martian Moons eXploration is JAXA's plan to land on Phobos, scoop ~10 g of regolith, and bring it home. The samples may settle a long-debated question: are Phobos and Deimos captured asteroids, or fragments from an ancient impact on Mars itself? The mission also includes a German-French rover for Phobos surface mobility — a wholly different operations model from anything the Hayabusa missions did at Itokawa or Ryugu. Launch was planned for 2024 but slipped to 2026 due to delays in JAXA's H3 launch vehicle.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: H3-24L from Tanegashima — JAXA's new heavy lifter.

events.label: MARS ORBIT INSERTION
events.note: Capture into Mars orbit; transfer to Phobos quasi-orbit over following months.

events.label: ROVER DEPLOY
events.note: DLR/CNES rover (~25 kg) released onto Phobos surface for in-situ studies.

events.label: PHOBOS SAMPLE
events.note: MMX touches down on Phobos; ~10 g of regolith collected.

events.label: EARTH RETURN
events.note: Sample re-entry capsule arrives at Earth ~2031.
